content.problemStatement:
Given an array AAA of length NNN.

You are allowed to perform the following operation on the array AAA **atmost** 202020 times:


Select a **non-empty** subsequence SSS of the array [1,2,3,…,N][1,2,3,\ldots,N][1,2,3,…,N] and an integer XXX (0≤X≤106)(0 \leq X \leq 10^6)(0≤X≤106);
Change AiA_iAi​ to Ai+XA_i+XAi​+X for all i∈Si \in Si∈S.
You have to sort the array AAA in **strictly increasing** order by performing **atmost** 202020 operations.

It is guaranteed that we can always sort the array AAA under given constraints.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN - the length of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1,A_2,\ldots,A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ representing the initial array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case print (2⋅Q+1)(2\cdot Q+1)(2⋅Q+1) lines. Here QQQ denotes the number of operations you performed to sort the array AAA. Note that, QQQ must be less than or equal to 202020. For each test case,



In the first line, print QQQ, the number of operations you perform.
Then, print 2⋅Q2 \cdot Q2⋅Q lines. The (2⋅i−1)th(2\cdot i-1)^{th}(2⋅i−1)th and (2⋅i)th(2\cdot i)^{th}(2⋅i)th of these lines denote the ithi^{th}ith (1≤i≤Q)(1 \le i \le Q)(1≤i≤Q) operation.

To describe the ithi^{th}ith operation, print two space-separated integers Ki(1≤Ki≤N)K_i(1 \leq K_i \leq N)Ki​(1≤Ki​≤N) and Xi(0≤Xi≤106)X_i(0 \leq X_i \leq 10^6)Xi​(0≤Xi​≤106) on (2⋅i−1)th({2 \cdot i -1})^{th}(2⋅i−1)th line and print KiK_iKi​ space-separated integers describing subsequence SiS_iSi​ on (2⋅i)th({2 \cdot i})^{th}(2⋅i)th line.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We can sort the array using 222 operations:

First operation: Choose S=[2]S = [2]S=[2] and X=1X = 1X=1. Thus, we perform A2=A2+1A_2 = A_2+1A2​=A2​+1. The array becomes [4,5,4][4, 5, 4][4,5,4].
Second operation: Choose S=[3]S = [3]S=[3] and X=2X = 2X=2. Thus, we perform A3=A3+2A_3 = A_3 + 2A3​=A3​+2. The final array is [4,5,6][4,5,6][4,5,6].

The array is now sorted in strictly increasing order.
Test case 222: We can sort the array using 444 operations:

First operation: Choose S=[2]S = [2]S=[2] and X=20X = 20X=20. Thus, we perform A2=A2+20A_2 = A_2+20A2​=A2​+20. The array becomes [8,28,5,2,1][8, 28, 5, 2, 1][8,28,5,2,1].
Second operation: Choose S=[3]S = [3]S=[3] and X=30X = 30X=30. Thus, we perform A3=A3+30A_3 = A_3 + 30A3​=A3​+30. The array becomes [8,28,35,2,1][8, 28, 35, 2, 1][8,28,35,2,1].
Third operation: Choose S=[4]S = [4]S=[4] and X=40X = 40X=40. Thus, we perform A4=A4+40A_4 = A_4+40A4​=A4​+40. The array becomes [8,28,35,42,1][8, 28, 35, 42, 1][8,28,35,42,1].
Fourth operation: Choose S=[5]S = [5]S=[5] and X=50X = 50X=50. Thus, we perform A5=A5+50A_5 = A_5 + 50A5​=A5​+50. The final array is [8,28,35,42,51][8, 28, 35, 42, 51][8,28,35,42,51].

The array is now sorted in strictly increasing order.
Test case 333: The array is already sorted. Thus, we require 000 operation.
